product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — worker workRelationships child collection
interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM, resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

service.authentication: basic auth in this fixture: Authorization: Basic base64(user:password) on EVERY request

required_headers.REST-Framework-Version: 4 — selects the structured error document; sent on every request

query_serialization.order: finder, q, fields, onlyData, orderBy, totalResults, limit, offset — unset options omitted; false booleans omitted
query_serialization.finder: finder=<name>[;<var>=<value>[,<var>=<value>]] — ';' after the name and ',' between variables literal; variable VALUES percent-encoded (space -> %20, single quote -> %27)
query_serialization.q: q=<expr>[;<expr>] — expressions joined with ';' then percent-encoded: '=' -> %3D, '>' -> %3E, ';' -> %3B, space -> %20, and single quotes -> %27 (pre-encoded so WHATWG URL query normalization cannot rewrite the request line)
query_serialization.finder_q_combination: finder and q are independent query parameters; when both are supplied both are emitted, finder first

collection_envelope.shape: { items: [...], count, hasMore, limit, offset, totalResults?, links? }
collection_envelope.server_clamp: this pod clamps any requested limit to 3; the RESPONSE limit/count are authoritative — next offset = previous offset + returned count
collection_envelope.loop_condition: hasMore, and only hasMore; totalResults (fixture: a bogus 999 estimate) is surfaced but never bounds the walk
collection_envelope.unstable_totals: rows can be inserted between page fetches, shifting offsets — the walker deduplicates by PeriodOfServiceId and reports duplicatesSkipped; rows that slide before the current offset are legitimately missed by offset paging

link_normalization.item_links: each item carries links [{rel: self|canonical, href: <absolute url>, name, kind}]
link_normalization.rule: normalized items expose id (PeriodOfServiceId), data (attributes with links removed), and self/canonical as PATH-ONLY forms of the absolute hrefs

checkpointing.store: single JSON file mapping key -> { offset, seenIds }
checkpointing.key: collection path + '?' + the full query string WITHOUT the offset parameter — a different q or finder never reuses another walk's checkpoint
checkpointing.failure: a failing page surfaces OraclePageError AFTER the checkpoint (failed page's offset + every delivered id) is durably written
checkpointing.resume: a fresh store over the same file resumes at the checkpointed offset, skips ids in seenIds (the pod may re-serve a delivered row), and clears the key on completion

error_document.fixture_error: 500 Internal Server Error with o:errorCode ORA-04021; every detail entry preserved; credentials never appear in error text

mock_fixture.origin: loopback http://127.0.0.1:{port} standing in for the Fusion pod
